questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A tank filled with water has two taps to exhaust and pour. A hollow spherical ball is half submerged in water. Through one tap, water is taken out and through another tap, a liquid of density double the density of water is poured in tank such that volume of liquid in tank remains constant. Sphere will
questions.solutionText: Solution Given that the hollow spherical ball is half-submerged in water. To satisfy the condition of floatation of a hollow spherical ball, the weight of the ball must be balanced by the upward force i.e., the buoyant force or upthrust acting by the fluid on the ball. Thus, W = Upthrust . We know that upthrust is given as F b = V i × ρ l × g , here V i is the volume of liquid displaced, ρ l is the density of liquid and g is the acceleration due to gravity. From the above relation, we get V i = W ρ l g . Now, if the density of liquid ρ l is increased, then immersed volume V i will decrease and the ball will go up.

Segment 1: ( 0 , 0 ) → ( 4 , 20 ) — triangle W 1 = 1 2 × 4 × 20 = 40 J Segment 2: ( 4 , 20 ) → ( 8 , 20 ) — rectangle W 2 = 4 × 20 = 80 J Segment 3: ( 8 , 20 ) → ( 12 , − 20 ) — trapezoid crossing zero F goes from 20 to − 20 linearly. It crosses zero at x = 10 . W 3 = 1 2 × 2 × 20 + 1 2 × 2 × ( − 20 ) = 20 − 20 = 0 J Total work = 40 + 80 + 0 = 120 J Answer: (2) 120 J

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Identify the correct statements from the following: (A) Work done by a man in lifting a bucket out of a well by means of a rope tied to the bucket is negative. (B) Work done by gravitational force in lifting a bucket out of a well by a rope tied to the bucket is negative. (C) Work done by friction on a body sliding down an inclined plane is positive. (D) Work done by an applied force on a body moving on a rough horizontal plane with uniform velocity in zero. (E) Work done by the air resistance on an oscillating pendulum in negative. Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solutionText: Solution A) In the process of lifting a bucket from a well, the man does positive work, as the force and displacement are in the same direction. B) In the process of lifting a bucket from a well, gravitational force does negative work, as the force and displacement are in opposite directions. C) Friction does negative work on a block sliding down an incline, as it acts opposite to the motion. D) Work done by the applied force is non-zero, as it balances friction to maintain uniform velocity. E) Air resistance does negative work on an oscillating pendulum, as it acts opposite to the direction of motion.

questions.text: In the given figure, the capacitance C 1 , C 3 , C 4 , C 5 have a capacitance 4 μ F each. If the capacitor C 2 has a capacitance 10 μ F , then effective capacitance between A and B will be
questions.solutionText: Solution Capacitance of capacitors C 1 , C 3 , C 4 , C 5 = 4 μ F each and capacitance of capacitor C 2 = 10 μ F . If a battery is applied across A and B , the points b and c will be at the same potential (since C 1 = C 4 = C 3 = C 5 = 4 μ F ). Therefore no charge flows through C 2 . We have the capacitors C 1 and C 5 in series. Therefore their equivalent capacitance, C ′ = C 1 × C 5 C 1 + C 5 = 4 × 4 4 + 4 = 2 μ F Similarly, C 4 and C 3 are in series. Therefore their equivalent capacitance, C ′ ′ = C 3 × C 4 C 3 + C 4 = 4 × 4 4 + 4 = 2 μ F . Now C ′ and C ′ ′ are in parallel. Therefore effective capacitance between A and B = C ′ + C ′ ′ = 2 + 2 = 4 μ F .

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: Among the alkali metal hydrides, which of the following is the most thermally stable, and what is the fundamental reason behind this stability?
questions.solutionText: Solution Among alkali metal hydrides, thermal stability decreases down the group: LiH > NaH > KH > RbH > CsH. LiH is the most thermally stable (decomposes around 900 ∘ C). This is because Li + is the smallest alkali metal cation, and H − is also relatively small. The comparable small sizes lead to a very high lattice enthalpy, making the ionic lattice extremely strong and difficult to break. Answer: (C) LiH; the comparable small sizes of the cation and anion result in a very high lattice enthalpy.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: Ozone-layer is getting depleted because of
questions.solutionText: Solution Ozone-layer is getting depleted because of excessive use of man-made compounds containing both fluorine and chlorine. The main reason being CFC molecules are made up of chlorine, fluorine and carbon atoms which are extremely stable. This extreme stability allows CFC's to slowly make their way into the stratosphere (most molecules decompose before they can cross into the stratosphere from the troposphere). This prolonged life in the atmosphere allows them to reach great altitudes where photons are more energetic. When the CFC's come into contact with these high energy photons, their individual components are freed from the whole. Cl + O 3 → ClO + O 2 ClO + O 3 → Cl + 2 O 2

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: For the reaction H 2 + I 2 → 2 HI , the rate of disappearance of iodine is 10 - 6 mol L – 1 s - 1 . What will be the rate of formation of HI ?
questions.solutionText: Solution The given reaction is, H 2 + I 2 → 2 HI For this reaction, - d I 2 dt = 1 2 d HI dt Here, - d I 2 dt is the rate of disappearance of I 2 and d HI dt is rate of appearance of HI . So, d HI dt = - 2 d I 2 dt d HI dt = 2 × 10 - 6 The rate of the appearance of HI is 2 × 10 - 6 mol L – 1 s - 1 .

questions.subject: English & Logical Reasoning
questions.text: Find out the error in the following sentence: Neither she nor we takes the decision.
questions.solutionText: Solution The correct sentence will be "neither she nor we take the decision". When the subjects are joined by 'either-or', 'neither-nor', etc, they are considered as different persons and the verb agrees with the nearer subject. This is known as the proximity rule. Here, 'we' is the nearer plural, first-person subject. So, the verb must agree with the person and number of the subject. So, it will be the plural verb, 'take'.

questions.subject: English & Logical Reasoning
questions.text: A large solid cube is constructed by assembling 125 smaller identical cubes. The large cube is painted Red on two opposite faces, Blue on two adjacent faces, and Yellow on the remaining two faces. How many of the smaller cubes have exactly two faces painted, where exactly one of those faces is Yellow?
questions.solutionText: Solution n = 125 3 = 5 . Edge cubes with exactly 2 painted faces: n − 2 = 3 per edge. Total 12 edges. Face color assignment: Red (opposite): Top and Bottom Blue (adjacent): Front and Right Yellow (remaining): Back and Left (also adjacent) We need edges where exactly one of the two faces is Yellow. Top face (Red) meets: Front (Blue), Right (Blue), Back (Yellow), Left (Yellow) ⇒ 2 Red-Yellow edges Bottom face (Red) meets: Front (Blue), Right (Blue), Back (Yellow), Left (Yellow) ⇒ 2 Red-Yellow edges Vertical edges (side faces meeting each other): Front-Right: Blue-Blue (no Yellow) Right-Back: Blue-Yellow (1 valid edge) Back-Left: Yellow-Yellow (both Yellow, not exactly one) Left-Front: Yellow-Blue (1 valid edge) Total valid edges = 2 + 2 + 2 = 6 Total cubes = 6 × 3 = 18 Answer: (2) 18

questions.text: Let ω ≠ 1 be a cube root of unity and S be the set of all non-singular matrices of the form [ 1 a b ω 1 c ω 2 ω 1 ] where each of a , b and c is either ω or ω 2 , then the number of distinct matrices in the set S is
questions.solutionText: Solution Let A = [ 1 a b ω 1 c ω 2 ω 1 ] For non-singular matrix | A | ≠ 0 ⇒ | 1 a b ω 1 c ω 2 ω 1 | ≠ 0 ⇒ 1 ( 1 − ω c ) − a ( ω − ω 2 c ) + b ( 0 ) ≠ 0 ⇒ 1 ( 1 − ω c ) − a ω ( 1 − ω c ) ≠ 0 ⇒ ( 1 − ω c ) ( 1 − a ω ) ≠ 0 ⇒ c ≠ 1 ω and a ≠ 1 ω ⇒ c ≠ ω 2 and a ≠ ω 2 … [ ∵ ω 3 = 1 ] So possible value of a and c is ω only and b can take values ω or ω 2 . ∴ The possible number of distinct matrices = 2 .

questions.text: Let a → , b → and c → be three non zero vectors such that b → · c → = 0 and a → × ( b → × c → ) = b → - c → 2 . If d → be a vector such that b → · d → = a → · b → , then ( a → × b → ) · ( c → × d → ) is equal to
questions.solutionText: Solution Given, a → × b → × c → = b → - c → 2 Using the vector triple cross product we have, a → × b → × c → = a → · c → b → - a → · b → c → So, ( a → · c → ) b → - ( a → · b → ) c → = b → - c → 2 On comparing both sides we get, a → · c → = 1 2 , a → · b → = 1 2 ∵ b → · d → = a → · b → ∴ b → · d → = 1 2 Using vector cross-product formulae we have ( a → × b → ) · ( c → × d → ) = a → · ( b → × ( c → × d → ) ) = a → · ( ( b → · d → ) c → - ( b → · c → ) d → ) ∵ b → · c → = 0 = ( a → · c → ) ( b → · d → ) = 1 2 × 1 2 = 1 4

questions.solutionText: Solution The intersection point of line y = 7 x - 25 and circle x 2 + y 2 = 25 is x 2 + 7 x - 25 2 = 25 ⇒ 50 x 2 - 350 x + 600 = 0 ⇒ x - 3 x - 4 = 0 ⇒ x = 3 , x = 4 ⇒ y = - 4 , 3 ∴ Coordinates of A 3 , - 4 and B 4 , 3 ∴ Distance between A and B = 4 - 3 2 + 3 + 4 2 = 5 2 Alternate Required distance = 2 a 2 1 + m 2 - c 2 1 + m 2 = 2 25 1 + 49 - 625 1 + 49 = 5 2

questions.text: The equation of the curve satisfying the differential equation x 2 d y = 2 - y d x and passing through P 1 , 4 is
questions.solutionText: Solution The given equation is d y d x = 2 - y x 2 ⇒ d y d x + y 1 x 2 = 2 x 2 Integrating factor = e ∫ d x x 2 = e - 1 x ∴ the solution is y e - 1 x = ∫ 2 x 2 e - 1 x d x Let - 1 x = t d x x 2 = d t i.e. y ⋅ e - 1 x = 2 ∫ e t d t ⇒ y e - 1 x = 2 e t + c ⇒ y e - 1 x = 2 e - 1 x + c ⇒ y = 2 + c ⋅ e 1 x Since, the curve is passing through P 1 , 4 ⇒ 2 e = c ⇒ y = 2 + 2 e ⋅ e 1 x

questions.text: The arithmetic means of a set of observations is x ¯ . If each observation is divided by b and then it is increased by 12 , then the mean of the new series is

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: For N = 12600 , the number of even divisors is
questions.solutionText: Solution On prime factorization we have, N = 12600 = 2 3 3 2 5 2 7 1 . Number of factors = 3 + 1 2 + 1 2 + 1 1 + 1 = 72 . Removing power of 2 remaining factors which are odd = 2 + 1 2 + 1 1 + 1 = 18 . ∴ Even factors = 72 - 18 = 54 . Hence, the answer is 54 .

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: If α and β are two distinct complex numbers such that | α | = | β | and Re ( α ) > 0 , Im ( β ) < 0 , then α + β α − β may be:
questions.solutionText: Solution Since | α | = | β | , both lie on a circle of radius r centered at origin. Let α = r e i θ 1 , β = r e i θ 2 α + β α − β = e i θ 1 + e i θ 2 e i θ 1 − e i θ 2 Factor out e i ( θ 1 + θ 2 ) / 2 : Numerator: 2 cos ⁡ θ 1 − θ 2 2 ⋅ e i ( θ 1 + θ 2 ) / 2 Denominator: 2 i sin ⁡ θ 1 − θ 2 2 ⋅ e i ( θ 1 + θ 2 ) / 2 α + β α − β = cos ⁡ θ 1 − θ 2 2 i sin ⁡ θ 1 − θ 2 2 = 1 i cot ⁡ θ 1 − θ 2 2 = − i cot ⁡ θ 1 − θ 2 2 This is purely imaginary (provided θ 1 ≠ θ 2 and cot is real, which holds since α ≠ β ). The conditions Re ( α ) > 0 and Im ( β ) < 0 simply ensure α ≠ β and the expression is well-defined. Answer: (1) purely imaginary

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: In a group of 400 people, 160 are smokers and non-vegetarian; 100 are smokers and vegetarian and the remaining 140 are non-smokers and vegetarian. Their chances of getting a particular chest disorder are 35 % , 20 % and 10 % respectively. A person is chosen from the group at random and is found to be suffering from the chest disorder. The probability that the selected person is a smoker and non-vegetarian is :
questions.solutionText: Solution Consider following events A : Person chosen is a smoker and non vegetarian. B : Person chosen is a smoker and vegetarian. C : Person chosen is a non-smoker and vegetarian E : Person chosen has a chest disorder Given P A = 160 400 P B = 100 400 P C = 140 400 P E A = 35 100 P E B = 20 100 P E C = 10 100 To find P A E = P A P E A P A · P E A + P B · P E B + P C · P E C = 160 400 × 35 100 160 400 × 35 100 + 100 400 × 20 100 + 140 400 × 10 100 = 28 45 option 4

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: What is the area of the region bounded by the parabolas y 2 = 4 x and x 2 = 4 y ?
questions.solutionText: Solution Intersection points: y 2 = 4 x and x 2 = 4 y From x 2 = 4 y ⇒ y = x 2 / 4 . Substituting into y 2 = 4 x : x 4 / 16 = 4 x ⇒ x 4 = 64 x ⇒ x ( x 3 − 64 ) = 0 ⇒ x = 0 , x = 4 Upper curve: y = 2 x (from y 2 = 4 x ) Lower curve: y = x 2 / 4 (from x 2 = 4 y ) Area = ∫ 0 4 ( 2 x − x 2 4 ) d x = [ 4 x 3 / 2 3 − x 3 12 ] 0 4 = 4 ( 8 ) 3 − 64 12 = 32 3 − 16 3 = 16 3 Answer: (2) 16 3 sq. units

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: A bag contains 4 red and 6 black balls. A ball is drawn at random from the bag, its color is observed and this ball along with two additional balls of the same color are returned to the bag. If now a ball is drawn at random from the bag, then the probability that this drawn ball is red, is:
questions.solutionText: Solution Required Probability = P(red, red) + P(black, red) = 4 4 + 6 × 6 4 + 6 + 2 + 6 6 + 4 × 4 6 + 4 + 2 = 24 + 24 10 × 12 = 48 10 × 12 = 2 5
